subject: UX & Research
title: The Snapchat Redesign Revolt
description: A fast breakdown of The Snapchat Redesign Revolt for builders.

hook.text: In 2018, Snapchat redesigned its entire interface. 1.2 million users signed a petition to reverse it.

buildup.text: The redesign mixed friends' stories with publisher content. Users couldn't find their friends' snaps anymore.

discovery.text: Kylie Jenner tweeted she didn't open Snapchat anymore. The stock dropped $1.3 billion in one day.

twist.text: Snap partially reversed the redesign, separating friends from publishers. But trust was already damaged.

climax.text: Users had a strong mental model: friends left, discover right. Breaking it broke the product's core appeal.

punchline.text: Respect how users navigate. Rearranging everything breaks trust.

quiz.question: What caused the Snapchat redesign backlash?
options: Mixing friends' content with publisher content broke user expectations
The app changed its color scheme
